reasoning: Time series forecasting of bank cash assets shows that in recent years, due to quantitative tightening (QT), there has been a general downward pressure on the data series, resulting in a slightly negative drift. Because weekly bank cash assets behave similarly to a highly volatile random walk, and absent recent real-time liquidity changes (such as Treasury General Account fluctuations or Fed ON RRP facility movements), my base assumption is slightly below 50% for an increase. True to the prompt's instruction that uncertainty should increase with forecast horizons (meaning probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5), I gradually adjust the probability from 0.48 at a one-week horizon to 0.50 at the three-month horizon.
